action.reasoning:
Observation: Eval submitted but failed with 'Render failed' messages. Looking carefully at the error stream, it says 'Evaluation failed' but the visible output shows it processing objects with some render failures. The eval crashed somewhere. The shape mismatch (e.g. 65476 vs 65536) is happening when I flatten rays to (N,3) but the renderer expects (H,W,3) - or the renderer's internal rays_valid filter creates mismatch with chunking.

Looking at TSR renderer: it does rays_o[rays_valid] type filtering. Some rays may be filtered out causing shape mismatch with rays_d. The bug is in my flattening - the renderer's _forward expects original shape and reshapes internally.

Actually, looking at the errors more carefully: 65476/65364/65439/65296/65534/65237 etc - these are all just under 65536. This is a known TripoSR bug when chunk_size > some boundary. Let me set chunk_size much smaller to avoid the issue. Or use the model.render() built-in path.

Let me look at how model.render works internally - maybe I should pass rays as (H,W,3) and let renderer handle chunking properly. Currently I flatten which may break invariants.

Decision: Use (H,W,3) rays and reduce chunk_size. Also add try/except per view with fallback to blank image. Submit eval since we need a baseline.

Actually I already submitted but got 'Evaluation failed'. The error output is truncated but seems eval crashed. Let me check - the task likely failed because some renders are missing files. Let me make predict.py super robust: always write a blank image first, then try to render and overwrite. That way every view file exists.

Also let me debug locally first to ensure no crashes.
